records.ety: [Akin to E. wild, Dan. forvilde to bewilder, Icel. villr bewildered, villa to bewilder; cf. AS. wildor a wild animal. See Wild, a., and cf. Wilderness.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Wildered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Wildering.]

quotes: Long lost and wildered in the maze of fate.
Again the wildered fancy dreams Of spouting fountains, frozen as they rose.
